article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor of the transverse colon with peritoneal metastasis: a case report
label: Fig. 1
caption: Computed tomography scan showing a large, well-circumscribed, heterogenous mass arising from the transverse colon

article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor of the transverse colon with peritoneal metastasis: a case report
label: Fig. 2
caption: a Gross specimen showing a large, globular tumor. b Cut surface showing the presence of solid gray-white areas along with areas of hemorrhage and cystic change

article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor of the transverse colon with peritoneal metastasis: a case report
label: Fig. 3
caption: a Hematoxylin and eosin sections showing sheets and fascicles of elongated to spindle-shaped tumor cells (× 4). b Tumor cells having elongated hyperchromatic nuclei and mild to moderate amount of cytoplasm, showing moderate degree of pleomorphism and atypia (× 20). c Tumor reaching up to inked serosal resected margin (× 4). d Tumor necrosis (× 4). e Atypical mitosis (× 40)

article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor of the transverse colon with peritoneal metastasis: a case report
label: Fig. 4
caption: Immunohistochemistry showing patchy positivity for S-100

article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor of the transverse colon with peritoneal metastasis: a case report
label: Fig. 5
caption: Diagnostic approach to malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor of the colon. GANT gastrointestinal autonomic tumor, GIST gastrointestinal stromal tumor, H&E hematoxylin and eosin, IHC immunohistochemistry, MPNST malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor, SMA smooth muscle actin

article_title: A Rare Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor of the Maxilla Mimicking a Periapical Lesion
label: Figure 1
caption: Periapical radiograph showing radiopaque image of the roots of teeth 21 and 22, which are compatible with root canal filling material, obtained from a radiolucent image of the blurred boundaries in the region of the upper incisors.

article_title: A Rare Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor of the Maxilla Mimicking a Periapical Lesion
label: Figure 2
caption: (a) Cell bundle arrangements with rounded, large nuclei that sometimes contain palisades, strands, and/or hyalinized islands. Hematoxylin and eosin (HE) staining, 40x. (b) Spindle cells with comma-shaped nuclei. HE staining, 100x. (c) Cells positive for S-100 protein. IHC, 200x. (d) Cells positive for glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP). IHC, 200x.

article_title: A Rare Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor of the Maxilla Mimicking a Periapical Lesion
label: Figure 3
caption: (a) CT panoramic reconstruction of maxilla. (b) CT axial reconstruction of the jaw. (c) CT coronal reconstruction of anterior region of maxilla. (d) CT sagittal reconstruction of tooth 11 region.

article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor with extensive osteosarcomatous and chondrosarcomatous differentiation: A case report
journal: International Journal of Surgery Case Reports
label: Fig. 1
caption: Magnetic resonance imaging coronal view (panel A) and sagittal view (panel B) showing clearly the lesion with some calcification.

article_title: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor with extensive osteosarcomatous and chondrosarcomatous differentiation: A case report
journal: International Journal of Surgery Case Reports
label: Fig. 2
caption: An operative view showing the lesion as it was dissected from the surrounding structure (Panel A). Panel B showing the excised mass which looked well encapsulated with a smooth surface.
